$comment: Ollama Agent — runs entirely locally with no GitHub Copilot auth required.
This example shows how to point the wrapper at a locally-running Ollama instance
instead of the GitHub Copilot API (BYOK: Bring Your Own Key).
IMPORTANT — model requirements:
The agent runtime drives an agentic loop that depends on NATIVE tool calling
(function calling). The model MUST support structured tool calls, not just text.
Small/older models (e.g. qwen2.5-coder:7b) emit tool calls as plain-text JSON
and break the loop — you'll see a raw {"name":...} blob instead of an answer.
Use a model with robust tool-calling support. Verified working: qwen3.6.
See the 'Bring Your Own Model (BYOK)' section of the README for details.
Prerequisites:
1. Install Ollama:  https://ollama.com
2. Pull a model:    ollama pull qwen3.6
3. Start Ollama:    ollama serve   (it listens on http://localhost:11434 by default)
To run:
./agents/ollama/start.sh start
To use a different model, change copilot.model and make sure you have pulled it,
and that it supports tool calling:
ollama pull llama3.3
# then set "model": "llama3.3" below
You can also override model and provider entirely via environment variables,
using the same vars as the gh-copilot CLI:
export COPILOT_PROVIDER_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434/v1
export COPILOT_MODEL=qwen3.6
./agents/ollama/start.sh start

agentCard.name: Ollama Agent
agentCard.description: A locally-running A2A agent powered by Ollama. No GitHub Copilot account required.

skills.name: General Assistant
skills.description: Answer questions, reason through problems, and assist with general tasks using a locally-running model

server.$comment: Using port 3002 so this agent can run alongside the example (3000) and filesystem-assistant (3001).

$comment: provider — points the SDK at your local Ollama instance instead of GitHub Copilot.
type: 'openai'  — Ollama exposes an OpenAI-compatible API.
baseUrl         — Ollama's default endpoint. Append /v1 for the OpenAI-compatible path.
wireApi         — Use 'completions' (Chat Completions API) for broadest Ollama model
compatibility. Avoid 'responses' for Ollama: many models reject it
with a 'does not support thinking' error.
No apiKey is needed — Ollama does not require authentication.
model — must match a model you pulled with 'ollama pull' (run 'ollama list').
It MUST support native tool calling (see the $comment block at the top
of this file and the README BYOK section). Verified working: qwen3.6.
copilot.systemPrompt: You are a helpful local AI assistant. Answer questions clearly and concisely. You run entirely on the user's local machine via Ollama.

timeouts.$comment: Local models can be slower than cloud APIs, especially on first run. 300 s gives headroom.

logging.$comment: Use 'debug' if you need to trace SDK-level events during development.
